newsArray.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to Use Separate Changing Room During England Tour Under ICC Safeguarding Rules
newsArray.description: India's 15-year-old batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is set to become the country's youngest international cricketer during the Ireland series before touring England. Under International Cricket Council safeguarding rules, he will use separate changing facilities and travel with his parents, highlighting the special measures surrounding one of cricket's brightest young talents.

newsArray.description: Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will use a separate dressing room from his India teammates during the upcoming five-match T20I series in England as part of mandatory child safeguarding regulations for under-16 players. newsArray.title: Ben Stokes Apologizes to Teammates Ahead of Third Test Against New Zealand
newsArray.description: England's cricket captain Ben Stokes has publicly apologized to his teammates for missing the second Test against New Zealand due to a nightclub incident. He acknowledged the unfairness of his absence, particularly affecting debutants, and expressed his support for stand-in captain Joe Root, who faced criticism during that match. Stokes also clarified his strong relationship with head coach Brendon McCullum, emphasizing their friendship and the importance of open communication. This apology comes as the team prepares for the crucial third Test, highlighting Stokes' leadership and commitment to his squad.
creator: narendra chaudhary
